title: Comparing means of two data sets
problem:
Two students recorded their daily screen time (in minutes) over five days.

Student A: $45, 60, 75, 90, 30$
Student B: $50, 65, 55, 80, 55$

Compare the mean screen time of each student and state who spent more time on screens on average.

steps.instruction: Calculate the mean for Student A.
Calculate the mean for Student B.
Compare the two means and state the conclusion.
steps.working: Student A: mean $= 60$ min. Student B: mean $= 61$ min. Since $61 > 60$, Student B spent more time on screens on average.

answer: Student B had a higher mean screen time of $61$ minutes, compared to Student A's mean of $60$ minutes.

title: Comparing spread using range and IQR
problem:
Two classes sat a maths quiz. Their sorted scores were:

Class A: $18, 22, 26, 29, 33, 36, 40$
Class B: $12, 20, 26, 30, 34, 38, 44$

Compare the spread of each class's scores using the range and IQR.

steps.instruction: Calculate the range for each class.
Find Q1 and Q3 for Class A.
steps.working: There are 7 values. The median is the 4th value: $29$. The lower half is $18, 22, 26$, so $\text{Q1} = 22$. The upper half is $33, 36, 40$, so $\text{Q3} = 36$.

steps.instruction: Find Q1 and Q3 for Class B.
steps.working: There are 7 values. The median is the 4th value: $30$. The lower half is $12, 20, 26$, so $\text{Q1} = 20$. The upper half is $34, 38, 44$, so $\text{Q3} = 38$.

steps.instruction: Calculate the IQR for each class.

steps.working: Class B has a greater range ($32 > 22$) and a greater IQR ($18 > 14$). Class B's scores are more spread out, while Class A's scores are more consistent.

answer: Class B has greater spread: range $= 32$ (vs $22$) and IQR $= 18$ (vs $14$). Class A is more consistent.

title: Writing a comparison using two statistics
problem:
Two athletes' 100 m sprint times (seconds) over a season are summarised below.

Athlete A: median $= 11.8$ s, IQR $= 0.4$ s, range $= 1.0$ s
Athlete B: median $= 12.3$ s, IQR $= 0.8$ s, range $= 1.6$ s

Compare the two athletes' performances and state which athlete is more consistent, justifying your answer with statistics.

steps.instruction: Compare the medians to assess typical performance.
steps.working: Athlete A has a lower median ($11.8$ s $< 12.3$ s). A lower sprint time means Athlete A is generally faster.

steps.instruction: Compare the IQRs to assess consistency.
steps.working: Athlete A has IQR $= 0.4$ s and Athlete B has IQR $= 0.8$ s. Since $0.4 < 0.8$, Athlete A's times are more tightly grouped -- Athlete A is more consistent.

steps.instruction: Compare the ranges to confirm the conclusion about spread.
steps.working: Athlete A has range $= 1.0$ s and Athlete B has range $= 1.6$ s. Since $1.0 < 1.6$, Athlete A also has less overall spread, confirming the conclusion.

steps.instruction: Write a comparison statement using at least two statistics.
steps.working: Athlete A has a lower median sprint time ($11.8$ s vs $12.3$ s) and a smaller IQR ($0.4$ s vs $0.8$ s), so Athlete A is both faster and more consistent than Athlete B.

answer: Athlete A is faster (lower median: $11.8$ s vs $12.3$ s) and more consistent (smaller IQR: $0.4$ s vs $0.8$ s).
